title: Custom LLM Models
summary: A hands-on path from 'an LLM is a black box' to fine-tuning your own Llama or Qwen and serving it behind a real API — the forward pass, prefill vs decode, the KV cache (with the memory formula worked by hand), quantization, LoRA/QLoRA, a full worked fine-tune, and production serving with vLLM.
note: The request arrived as one long user brief (train a custom LLM like Llama/Qwen, understand inference, inference servers, KV caching, and be able to implement it yourself). Rather than a single mega-page, this build keeps that brief as Concept 01 (source: user) and researches + reorganizes the whole ask into 10 coherent chapters in the Clear Mentor's voice, carrying one running example — the 'Acme Support Copilot' — through every idea. Content grounded in primary sources (LoRA, QLoRA, GQA, AWQ, PagedAttention papers; TRL/PEFT/vLLM docs; Llama & Qwen model cards).

concepts.title: What a Language Model Actually Is

images.prompt: A striking, modern editorial illustration in flat vector style: a glowing indigo-violet 'language model' rendered as a neat stack of translucent layer-slabs, fed from the left by a flowing ribbon of small cyan token-tiles and emitting a single bright green token-tile on the right. Beneath the stack, a warm industrial-amber GPU board with a small live-green status dot. A faint blueprint/tensor grid in the background. Cool near-white background, indigo-violet + amber + cyan palette with one green accent, generous whitespace, soft depth, no text baked in. Mood: a black box made legible.
images.alt: A glowing violet neural model block on the right, fed by a cyan stream of token tiles from the left, with a warm amber GPU beneath it, on a blueprint grid
images.caption: A model is just weights + a KV cache on a GPU — tokens in, one token out, on repeat.
